prerequisites: A Ticketmaster developer account, created by registering at the Ticketmaster Developer Portal (developer.ticketmaster.com).
An API key, which is issued on registration and is used to authorize all Discovery API requests.
The api_key configuration value, which is the only required field for this connector.

authMethods.name: API key (apikey query parameter)
authMethods.summary: Authentication uses a single API key. Provide it as the connector's required api_key secret field. Ticketmaster authorizes each request by reading the key from the apikey query parameter (for example https://app.ticketmaster.com/discovery/v2/events.json?apikey=YOUR_KEY). Without a valid API key the API returns a 401 status with an Invalid ApiKey error.

setupSteps.title: Register for a Ticketmaster developer account
setupSteps.body: Go to the Ticketmaster Developer Portal at developer.ticketmaster.com and register for an account (or log in). Registration is the prerequisite for accessing the Discovery API.

setupSteps.title: Obtain your API key
setupSteps.body: On successful registration you are issued an API key. Pass this key in the apikey query parameter of every request to the Discovery API.

setupSteps.title: Provide the API key to the connector
setupSteps.body: Enter your Ticketmaster API key into the connector's required api_key field. The connector sends it as the apikey query parameter on every request to the Discovery API root (https://app.ticketmaster.com/discovery/v2/).

setupSteps.title: Account for rate limits
setupSteps.body: The Discovery API enforces a default quota of 5,000 API calls per day and a rate limit of 5 requests per second. Deep paging is also limited so that size * page must be less than 1000. Keep sync frequency within these limits.
